info.name: Cisco Expressway Configuration API
info.description:
RESTful API for configuring and managing Cisco Expressway systems including zones, search rules, transforms, DNS servers, NTP servers, SFTP configuration, system upgrades, and admin account management. The API uses JSON Schema version 4 for request and response schemas and is self-documented via RAML definitions available at /api/raml on the Expressway system. All endpoints require HTTPS and HTTP Basic Authentication using Expressway administrator credentials.

Contact Support:
 Name: Cisco TAC
 Email: tac@cisco.com

request.description: Returns basic system information for the Expressway node including the system name, software version, hardware details, serial number, and uptime.

response.name: System information retrieved successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway List all neighbor zones

request.description: Retrieves all neighbor zones configured on the Expressway. Neighbor zones connect the local Expressway to another system such as another Expressway, Unified CM, or a gatekeeper. Supports filtering by name.

response.name: Neighbor zone list retrieved successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway Create a neighbor zone

request.description: Creates a new neighbor zone on the Expressway. Neighbor zones define connections to peer systems such as other Expressway nodes, Unified CM clusters, or gatekeepers with configurable H.323 and SIP protocol settings.

response.name: Neighbor zone created successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway List all search rules

request.description: Retrieves all search rules configured on the Expressway. Search rules define how the Expressway routes incoming search requests to the appropriate target zones or policy services, with configurable alias pattern matching, priority, and protocol filtering.

response.name: Search rule list retrieved successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway Create a search rule

request.description: Creates a new search rule on the Expressway. Search rules determine how incoming calls are routed to target zones based on alias patterns, source zones, and protocol type. Each search rule has a configurable priority that determines its evaluation order.

response.name: Search rule created successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway List all pre-search transforms

request.description: Retrieves all pre-search transforms configured on the Expressway. Pre-search transforms modify the destination alias in an incoming search request before any searches are sent to external zones, enabling alias normalization and rewriting.

response.name: Transform list retrieved successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway Create a pre-search transform

request.description: Creates a new pre-search transform on the Expressway. Transforms are applied to incoming search requests to modify the destination alias before routing decisions are made, using pattern matching and replacement strings.

item.name: Cisco Expressway List Expressway-E traversal server zones

request.description: Retrieves all traversal server zones configured on the Expressway-E. Traversal server zones enable the Expressway-E to provide firewall traversal services to traversal clients (Expressway-C). Supports filtering by name using the path pattern /zone/traversalserver/name/{zoneName}.

response.name: Traversal server zone list retrieved successfully

item.name: Cisco Expressway Trigger a system upgrade

request.description: Initiates a system software upgrade on the Expressway. The SFTP configuration must be set before triggering the upgrade. The upgrade process downloads the image via SFTP and optionally reboots the system automatically.

response.name: Upgrade initiated successfully
